top_stories.label: Top Stories
top_stories.ai_summary: The big overnight story is Ukraine hitting back hard - one of its largest-ever drone barrages reached Moscow itself, killing four near the capital and forcing Russia's biggest airport to divert flights. The WHO's Ebola emergency declaration in DR Congo and Uganda is now formally in effect, with cross-border response meetings underway. The hantavirus-hit cruise ship MV Hondius is due to dock in Rotterdam this morning for final disinfection, closing the on-board phase of the outbreak. Trump and Iran are inching closer to a deal via Pakistan, but the US naval blockade of Iranian ports is still in place.

stories.headline: Ukraine launches one of its largest drone strikes on Russia; debris hits Moscow's biggest airport
stories.summary: Ukraine fired one of its largest combined drone waves at Russia overnight on May 16-17, killing at least four people (three near Moscow) and wounding a dozen others. Russian air defences said they shot down 81 drones aimed at Moscow and more than 556 across the country, with debris landing at Vnukovo - Russia's largest airport - forcing flight diversions. Zelenskyy called the strikes 'entirely justified' retaliation for Russia's deadly May 13-14 missile barrage on Kyiv.

stories.headline: WHO declares Ebola outbreak in DR Congo and Uganda a global health emergency
stories.summary: The World Health Organization on May 17 formally declared the Bundibugyo-strain Ebola outbreak in DR Congo and Uganda a Public Health Emergency of International Concern. Eight laboratory-confirmed cases, 246 suspected cases and around 80 suspected deaths have been recorded so far, including two confirmed cases (one fatal) in Uganda's capital Kampala. There are no approved vaccines or treatments for the Bundibugyo strain, and Africa CDC is convening urgent cross-border response talks.

stories.headline: MV Hondius cruise ship docks in Rotterdam this morning to close out hantavirus outbreak
stories.summary: The Dutch expedition cruise ship at the centre of the global Andes hantavirus outbreak is scheduled to arrive in Rotterdam on the morning of May 18. The remaining essential crew and medical staff will disembark under Dutch RIVM quarantine protocols, and the vessel will be deep-cleaned before any return to service. Eight cases have been laboratory-confirmed and three people have died since the outbreak was first reported.

stories.headline: Trump-Iran talks edge toward deal via Pakistan; US naval blockade still in force
stories.summary: President Trump said late on May 17 that there had been 'very good talks over the last 24 hours' with Iran and that 'it's very possible we'll make a deal', though it was unclear whether the contact was direct or via Pakistani intermediaries. Tehran is still formulating its response to the latest US proposal. Until a deal is signed, the US naval blockade of Iranian ports remains in force and the Strait of Hormuz is effectively closed.

stories.headline: Trump-Xi summit ends with 'strategic stability' pledge and Boeing order, but Taiwan tensions linger
stories.summary: Trump and Xi wrapped up their two-day Beijing summit on May 15 by agreeing to a 'constructive China-US relationship of strategic stability' that Beijing wants to anchor the next three years. Trump told Fox News that China will order 200 Boeing jets - more than expected - and the trade truce reached last October is set to be extended. But the two sides told different versions of what they agreed on Taiwan, and China renewed its warning that mishandling the issue would put the relationship 'in great jeopardy'.

stories.headline: US Pentagon to pull around 5,000 troops out of Germany
stories.summary: The US Department of Defense announced it will withdraw roughly 5,000 of the approximately 39,000 American troops currently stationed in Germany. The Stryker Brigade based at Vilseck is among the units affected. The move comes as European NATO allies push to expand their own conventional forces in response to the continuing war in Ukraine.

singapore.label: Singapore
singapore.ai_summary: A relatively quiet morning at home: expect thundery showers later today as Singapore stays in its rainy mid-May pattern, and Senior Minister Lee Hsien Loong heads off on a five-day visit to Guangxi and Shanghai. The Communicable Diseases Agency is still monitoring the two Singapore residents from the MV Hondius - that ship docks in Rotterdam this morning. PM Wong's package of CDC vouchers and U-Save rebates to cushion energy-price shocks from the Strait of Hormuz crisis remains the cost-of-living talking point.

stories.headline: SM Lee Hsien Loong heads to Guangxi and Shanghai on five-day China visit
stories.summary: Senior Minister Lee Hsien Loong begins an official visit to Guangxi and Shanghai from May 18 to 22 at the invitation of Beijing. The trip comes days after the Trump-Xi summit and is expected to focus on economic ties, the Suzhou Industrial Park 30-year framework, and Singapore's role as a stable regional partner amid US-China tensions.

stories.headline: Thundery showers expected on most afternoons; warmer second half of May
stories.summary: The Meteorological Service Singapore says short-duration thundery showers are likely on most afternoons during the second fortnight of May, occasionally stretching into the evening. Sumatra squalls could bring widespread thundery showers and gusty winds in the pre-dawn and morning on a few days. Daytime highs will be 33-34 degC, with one or two days touching 35 degC. PUB urges residents to subscribe to flash flood alerts via Telegram and SMS.

stories.headline: CDA continues to monitor two MV Hondius passengers as ship reaches Rotterdam
stories.summary: The Communicable Diseases Agency is still tracking two Singapore residents who had been on board the hantavirus-hit MV Hondius. Both remain isolated at the National Centre for Infectious Diseases pending test results and would face 30 days of quarantine plus 45 days of phone surveillance if cleared. CDA reiterates that the public risk in Singapore stays low because the Andes virus needs sustained close contact to spread.

stories.headline: Government brings forward CDC vouchers and U-Save rebates to cushion energy bills
stories.summary: Prime Minister Lawrence Wong has confirmed that next year's Community Development Council (CDC) vouchers will be brought forward and cash payouts enhanced, with additional U-Save rebates for HDB households, as the Strait of Hormuz crisis pushes energy prices higher. Businesses 'most affected by higher energy prices' will also get more help. Officials describe the measures as a buffer rather than a full subsidy of higher bills.

stories.headline: Tripartite Jobs Council kicks off as Singapore overhauls AI re-skilling
stories.summary: The newly-formed Tripartite Jobs Council, announced at the May Day Rally, is up and running, with Workforce Singapore and SkillsFuture Singapore being merged into a new Skills and Workforce Development Agency (SWDA) jointly run by MOM and MOE. Wong said the government 'may not be able to protect every job, but we will protect every worker' as AI reshapes white-collar work.

wars.label: Wars & Conflicts
wars.ai_summary: The Russia-Ukraine front just escalated sharply: Kyiv answered the May 13-14 Kyiv apartment-block massacre with one of its largest drone strikes ever on Russia, with debris hitting Moscow's main airport overnight. The US-Iran ceasefire is officially shaky but Trump now says 'very good talks' have happened in the last 24 hours via Pakistan. Israel's killing of Hamas military chief Izz al-Din al-Haddad still hangs over Gaza, while the Israel-Lebanon truce holds for now. Watch for a possible US-Iran framework announcement this week.

theatres.name: Russia-Ukraine War
theatres.status: Active - Ukraine launches one of its largest drone strikes on Russia in retaliation

timeline.event: Russia launches full-scale invasion of Ukraine.
Russia launches a record 8,000+ drones at Ukraine, the highest monthly total since the war began.
Russia and Ukraine declare competing ceasefires; both immediately accuse each other of violations.
US brokers a 3-day ceasefire and 1,000-prisoner swap, running May 9-11.
Putin publicly suggests the war is 'coming to an end' and offers to meet Zelenskyy in a third country.
Truce expires; Russia fires more than 200 drones overnight at Ukraine.
Russia launches 800+ drones in waves; combined daytime strike on Kyiv begins.
Russian missile and drone barrage on Kyiv kills at least 24, including 3 children; nine-storey apartment block collapses in Darnytsia.
Ukraine strikes Russian gas facilities in Orenburg region, more than 1,500 km from the border, in retaliation.
Ukraine claims to have intercepted six Russian Kinzhal hypersonic missiles.
Ukraine launches one of its largest combined drone strikes on Russia; 81 drones shot down over Moscow, 556 across the country. Three killed near Moscow, debris hits Vnukovo airport.
Zelenskyy hails the strike as 'entirely justified' retaliation; Russian state media downplays damage as flights resume from Moscow airports.

theatres.name: US/Israel-Iran (Strait of Hormuz)
theatres.status: Active - Trump signals 'very good talks' via Pakistan; US naval blockade still in force

timeline.event: US and Israel launch joint airstrikes on Iran, killing Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei and several officials. War begins.
US-Iran ceasefire announced; Iran's foreign minister declares Strait of Hormuz open. Oil prices fall 11% in a single day.
Strait of Hormuz tensions reignite; US and Iranian forces exchange attacks on commercial shipping.
Iran attacks UAE; US sinks 7 small Iranian boats; commercial vessels attacked in the strait.
US and Iran begin work on a one-page, 14-point memo via Pakistani mediators to end the war.
First Chinese oil tanker attacked in the Strait of Hormuz as the conflict widens.
Iran seizes a tanker in the Gulf of Oman; US 'disables' two Iranian ships.
Trump says the ceasefire is on 'massive life support' after rejecting Tehran's counter-proposal.
Trump threatens new strikes if Iran doesn't accept a deal; both sides still talking via Pakistan.
Trump says 'very good talks' have happened in the last 24 hours; Iran reviewing the latest US proposal. US naval blockade of Iranian ports remains in place.

theatres.name: Israel-Gaza / Lebanon
theatres.status: Active - Lebanon truce holding; Gaza talks stalled over Hamas disarmament

timeline.event: Hamas-led attacks on Israel kill ~1,200 and take ~250 hostages, triggering the Gaza war.
Mohammed Sinwar killed; Izz al-Din al-Haddad takes over Hamas's military wing.
Body of the last Israeli hostage, Ran Gvili, recovered by Israeli forces in northern Gaza, clearing the way for second-phase talks.
Israeli strike in western Gaza City kills Haddad - the last surviving Oct 7 architect - alongside his wife and daughter. Seven killed in total.
Israel-Lebanon truce extended for a further 45 days.
IDF and Hamas both confirm Haddad's death; Hamas calls the loss 'profound'.
Second-phase ceasefire talks remain stalled; Hamas rejects 'crude' disarmament demand, Israel says it won't move forward without progress.
Lebanon truce still holding; humanitarian convoys continue into Gaza under the first-phase framework.

hantavirus.label: Hantavirus Outbreak
hantavirus.ai_summary: The MV Hondius outbreak is hitting its inflection point - the ship docks in Rotterdam this morning for its final disinfection, effectively closing the on-board chapter. The count holds at 8 laboratory-confirmed and 2 probable cases with 3 deaths, spread across 12 countries through former passengers. WHO and ECDC continue to call epidemic risk low because Andes virus needs close, sustained contact to spread, but contact tracing will run for weeks. Singapore's two cases are still in NCID isolation awaiting results.

stories.headline: MV Hondius docks in Rotterdam today; ship to be disinfected after global outbreak
stories.summary: The expedition cruise ship at the centre of the Andes hantavirus outbreak is scheduled to arrive at the Port of Rotterdam on the morning of May 18 sailing at 12 knots. The remaining essential crew and medical staff will disembark in a staggered process under Dutch RIVM quarantine protocols, and the vessel will then undergo deep cleaning before any return to service. Passengers had already disembarked at Tenerife on May 10-11.

stories.headline: WHO holds case count at 8 confirmed, 2 probable, 3 deaths across 12 countries
stories.summary: WHO's latest Disease Outbreak News (DON601) on May 17 keeps the figures at 8 laboratory-confirmed and 2 probable Andes hantavirus cases, with 3 deaths (two laboratory-confirmed). Former passengers are hospitalised or quarantined in Australia, Canada, France, Germany, the Netherlands, Saint Helena, Singapore, South Africa, Spain, Switzerland, Turkey, and the United States. Crew members still on board remain symptom-free.

stories.headline: Singapore's two MV Hondius passengers still isolated at NCID
stories.summary: The Communicable Diseases Agency continues to monitor two Singapore residents who had been on the MV Hondius. Both are isolated at the National Centre for Infectious Diseases pending results; if negative, they will face 30 days of quarantine followed by 45 days of phone surveillance. CDA says public risk in Singapore is low and there has been no community transmission.

stories.headline: ECDC keeps risk assessment unchanged: low for the general public
stories.summary: ECDC's May 17 update reiterates that the overall risk for the general population in the EU/EEA remains low. The agency notes Andes virus is the only hantavirus known to spread between people via sustained close contact and possibly small-particle aerosol, but onward transmission has not been observed outside the ship. Member states are tracking former passengers through their public health systems.

cybersecurity.label: CyberSecurity
cybersecurity.ai_summary: Two unpatched zero-days dominate the cycle: Cisco's maximum-severity Catalyst SD-WAN auth bypass (CVE-2026-20182, CVSS 10.0) just hit its CISA federal patch deadline, and Microsoft Exchange Server has a new actively-exploited XSS-via-email flaw (CVE-2026-42897, CVSS 8.1) with only an emergency mitigation, not a real patch, available. Federal agencies have to mitigate Exchange by May 29. Linux 'Dirty Frag' kernel root chains and Google's AI-built 2FA bypass round out the bad news for defenders.

stories.headline: Microsoft Exchange Server zero-day actively exploited via crafted email (CVE-2026-42897)
stories.summary: Microsoft warns that attackers are exploiting a new cross-site scripting flaw in on-prem Exchange Server's Outlook Web Access. Sending a specially crafted email is enough to run arbitrary JavaScript in a victim's browser when opened in OWA, affecting Exchange Server SE, 2019 and 2016. No real patch yet - Microsoft has shipped an Exchange Emergency Mitigation Service rule. CISA added the flaw to KEV on May 15 with a federal mitigation deadline of May 29.

stories.headline: Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN auth bypass (CVE-2026-20182) hits federal patch deadline
stories.summary: The maximum-severity Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Controller authentication bypass added to the CISA KEV list on May 14 reaches its federal civilian patch deadline today, May 17. The flaw lets remote attackers log in as a high-privileged internal user and use NETCONF to rewrite SD-WAN fabric configuration. Cisco's only remediation is the upgrade - there is no workaround. Active exploitation in the wild continues.

stories.headline: Google: hackers used AI to build first known zero-day 2FA bypass exploit
stories.summary: Google's Threat Intelligence Group says it identified a criminal threat actor using a commercial AI model to discover and write a zero-day exploit that bypasses two-factor authentication. The group was reportedly preparing a mass-exploitation campaign before Google's counter-discovery neutralised it. It is the first publicly confirmed case of AI-generated exploit code being used in a live operation.

stories.headline: Linux 'Dirty Frag' chain (CVE-2026-43284 / 43500) gives root on all major distros
stories.summary: Two page-cache write vulnerabilities in the Linux kernel, chained together as 'Dirty Frag' and tracked as CVE-2026-43284 and CVE-2026-43500, give unprivileged local users root on all major distributions. A working PoC has been released and patches are rolling out across Ubuntu, RHEL, Debian and Fedora. Microsoft's earlier 'Copy Fail' kernel flaw (CVE-2026-31431) remains on the CISA KEV list with active exploitation.

stories.headline: NGINX rewrite module heap overflow patched (CVE-2026-42945, CVSS 9.2)
stories.summary: A heap buffer overflow in the NGINX ngx_http_rewrite_module affecting versions 0.6.27 through 1.30.0 has been patched. F5 advises immediate upgrade as the bug is reachable in default configurations with a rewrite directive. Cloud and CDN operators running custom NGINX builds should review for unofficial backports.

stories.headline: Grafana discloses unauthorised access to GitHub environment
stories.summary: Grafana Labs has disclosed that an 'unauthorised party' obtained a token granting access to its GitHub environment, including the ability to download codebase contents. The company says it has revoked the token, rotated secrets and found no signs of customer data exposure. Investigations are ongoing into how the token was leaked.

stories.headline: Windows BitLocker 'YellowKey' zero-day PoC still in the wild, no patch
stories.summary: The anonymous researcher who released the BitLocker bypass dubbed 'YellowKey' (and the paired 'GreenPlasma' CTFMON privilege escalation) confirmed the proof-of-concept exploit remains functional against Windows 11 and Windows Server 2022/2025 a week after disclosure. YellowKey functions 'like a backdoor' to encrypted drives via the Windows Recovery Environment. Microsoft has not yet shipped a patch.
